subject: [RFC PATCH 08/40] mm: page_alloc: superpageblock metadata for 1GB anti-fragmentation

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new data structure called superpageblock, which groups pageblocks at a coarser granularity of 1GB to enable anti-fragmentation tracking. The superpageblock maintains counts of pageblocks by migratetype and a list_head for future organization by fullness category. This is done to steer unmovable/reclaimable allocations into already-tainted superpageblocks, preserving clean superpageblocks for 1GB hugepage allocation. The patch adds new definitions and data structures in the Linux kernel's mmzone.h and page_alloc.c files, with negligible overhead of ~48 bytes per superpageblock.
